title: Designing a Production LLM Pipeline
concept: A production architecture: SentencePiece tokenization -> Quantized MoE backbone -> RAG vector database retrieval -> Llama Guard content filters -> Continuous batch serving.

exercises.prompt: Why do we need a "pipeline" instead of just using a raw LLM model on its own?

options.text: Because raw models are not written in Python.
To handle safety, speed, and real-time database lookup.
To make the model completely shut down when not in use.

exercises.explain: A pipeline connects multiple steps like tokenization, database retrieval, model execution, and safety filtering to deliver a fast, safe user experience.

cards.title: From Model to Masterpiece
cards.body: Building an AI model is like building an engine. But to drive a car, you need wheels, fuel lines, brakes, and a steering wheel. Let's learn how to connect an LLM to real-world pipelines that are safe, fast, and smart!

cards.title: What is a Production Pipeline?
cards.body: A production LLM pipeline is a sequence of connected tools that process user inputs, retrieve accurate information, run efficient models, filter out bad content, and serve multiple users at the same time without crashing.

cards.title: The Pizza Parlor Pipeline
cards.body:
Imagine running a super-busy pizza parlor. Instead of having one giant chef cook everything, you have:
1. A receptionist who cuts orders into smaller words (Tokenization).
2. A smart filing cabinet to quickly look up recipes (RAG Vector DB).
3. A kitchen where only the specialized chef for that specific pizza steps up to cook (Quantized MoE).
4. A safety inspector checking the pizza before it goes out (Llama Guard).
5. A delivery driver who groups orders together to deliver them fast (Continuous Batching).

cards.title: AI Banking Assistant in Action
cards.body:
When you ask an AI assistant about your bank account balance:
1. SentencePiece turns your text into numbers.
2. The system retrieves your transaction history from a database.
3. A lightweight MoE model processes the query.
4. Llama Guard checks that no private passwords are leaked in the output.
5. The server sends the response instantly alongside thousands of other users' requests.

cards.title: The Model Isn't Everything!
cards.body:
Myth: You just need a large AI model to build a great app.
Reality: A model alone is slow, expensive, and can hallucinate. You need a pipeline with retrieval databases, safety filters, and smart batching to make it work reliably for real users.

cards.title: The Architecture Shift
cards.body: Let's compare a basic raw LLM with a complete production-grade pipeline.

right.text: Fast MoE, RAG database lookup, Llama Guard safety filters, and continuous batch serving.

left.text: Slow, expensive, struggles with real-time data, and might output unsafe answers.

exercises.prompt: Which technology ensures the AI server can handle thousands of users at the same time without long waiting delays?
exercises.explain: Continuous batching groups incoming requests dynamically as they arrive, maximizing GPU usage and speed.

exercises.prompt: Does quantizing a Mixture of Experts (MoE) model make it run faster and use less computer memory?
exercises.explain: Quantization shrinks the size of a model, making it run faster on cheaper hardware with minimal loss in quality.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the name of the safety tool used to filter out bad content in an LLM pipeline.
exercises.sentence: We use ________ to scan inputs and outputs for harmful or unsafe text.
exercises.explain: Llama Guard acts as a safety filter at the end of the pipeline to check inputs and outputs for harmful content.

exercises.prompt: Identify the true production-grade LLM pipeline among these choices.

options.text: An LLM that answers medical questions directly from its memory without any verification filters.
An LLM that fetches real medical papers from a database, answers using a fast MoE model, and checks safety using Llama Guard.
A python script that uses 'if/else' statements to reply to pre-written user messages.

exercises.explain: Option B is correct because it uses a real-world knowledge database (RAG) and safety checks, whereas Option A has no safety and Option C is just a simple hardcoded dictionary.

exercises.prompt: You are building a production-grade AI customer support agent. Which sequence represents the most complete, safe, and highly optimized pipeline from start to finish?

options.text: Tokenization -> Llama Guard -> RAG -> MoE -> Single-sequence batching
SentencePiece -> RAG Retrieval -> Quantized MoE -> Llama Guard -> Continuous Batching
Llama Guard -> Quantized MoE -> SentencePiece -> RAG Retrieval

exercises.explain: A production LLM pipeline requires multiple coordinated steps to be safe, fast, and smart: SentencePiece tokenizes, RAG retrieves external facts, MoE runs the model efficiently, Llama Guard filters content, and continuous batching optimizes throughput.
